paper.title_en: Performance Evaluation of Kinetic Inductance Detector on Yttria-Stabilized Zirconia Substrates With Tuned Yttria Concentrations
paper.abstract_en: This study evaluated the performance of lumped element kinetic inductance detectors (LEKIDs) fabricated on yttria-stabilized zirconia (YSZ) substrates with yttria concentrations of 9.5%, 13%, and 20% . We focus on the application of LEKIDs in the search for undiscovered double-beta decay on <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{94}$</tex-math></inline-formula>Zr and are developing a detector for this purpose. The yttria concentration of YSZ affects its properties, such as ionic conductivity, necessitating analysis of its effects for detector applications. In this work, LEKIDs made with niobium thin films were tested for resonant frequency, quality factor, and temperature-dependent behavior. No significant differences in yttria concentration were observed that would affect LEKID performance. However, temperature-control measurements confirmed a large two-level system contribution, regardless of the yttria concentration.
